candidate_problems.text: How can 'small criticism-stable region of possibility space' be operationalized so that structurally equivalent variants are distinguished from genuinely different explanations?
Are there good explanations in probabilistic or highly redundant domains where many nearby variations survive, making low swappability an unreliable marker of explanatory depth?
What perturbation set is appropriate for testing load-bearingness without making the result arbitrary to the chosen variation operators?
Can an explanation appear hard to vary only because it is underspecified at the right abstraction level, thereby passing perturbation tests without genuinely constraining the phenomenon?
